description: E2E test case designs for CROSS-MODULE integration scenarios - failures that span multiple modules

test_cases.title: Agent crashes during session save - storage consistency
test_cases.description: Agent crashes mid-execution while storage is writing - verify storage consistency
test_cases.risk_addressed: Storage left in inconsistent state after agent crash
steps: Storage is in middle of save() operation
Verify either full old state OR full new state (not partial)
expected_outcomes: Either rollback to previous state OR complete new state
oracle_checks: Storage load after crash returns consistent state

test_cases.title: Permission check race in parallel tool execution
test_cases.description: Two tools executing simultaneously - permission check between them has race window
test_cases.risk_addressed: Permission bypass via timing attack on race condition
test_cases.preconditions: Agent with ReadOnly scope, two tools called in parallel
steps: Timing analysis: ensure no window where WriteTool could have proceeded
expected_outcomes: No race condition allows restricted operation
Order of arrival doesn't determine outcome
oracle_checks: Both tools' permission checks complete before either executes

test_cases.title: Provider retries expose auth token in logs
test_cases.description: Failed provider request with auth header is retried - token may appear in retry logs
test_cases.risk_architecture: Provider -> HTTP Client -> Auth Token -> Logs
test_cases.risk_addressed: Auth token leaked during provider retry logging
test_cases.preconditions: Provider with Bearer token, request fails and is retried
steps: Configure provider with Bearer token auth
Check all logs for token exposure
oracle_checks: Retry mechanism doesn't log raw requests

test_cases.title: Session compaction loses messages during agent iteration
test_cases.description: Compactor runs while agent is reading messages - race condition
test_cases.risk_addressed: Messages lost or duplicated during compaction
test_cases.preconditions: Session with 100+ messages, agent mid-iteration reading messages
steps: Verify agent sees consistent message stream
expected_outcomes: Agent sees all messages it started reading OR none of them
No interleaving of old and compacted messages
No message appears twice or disappears
oracle_checks: Agent holds read lock while iterating
Write lock held only during atomic swap

test_cases.title: Server crash leaves storage transaction uncommitted
test_cases.description: HTTP request completes but storage commit happens after response sent - crash loses data
test_cases.risk_addressed: Response sent before storage commit - crash loses data
steps: Server crashes AFTER sending response but BEFORE storage commit
Verify session was NOT saved (transaction rolled back)
expected_outcomes: Either: response sent only AFTER commit (session exists after restart)
No: response says success but data not saved
oracle_checks: Storage commit happens before HTTP response

test_cases.title: Provider model change during active request
test_cases.description: Config hot-reload changes provider model mid-streaming-response
test_cases.risk_architecture: Config Hot Reload -> Provider Config -> Active Request
test_cases.risk_addressed: Model change causes inconsistent streaming response
steps: Verify entire response from same model
expected_outcomes: Config change does not affect in-flight requests
oracle_checks: Model snapshot taken at request start
Streaming not affected by config reload

test_cases.title: Git operation creates large diff - storage pressure
test_cases.description: Git commit with massive diff causes storage pressure
test_cases.risk_addressed: Large git diff exhausts storage or memory
steps: Session records tool invocation with diff
expected_outcomes: Large diff truncated or stored efficiently

test_cases.title: Shell tool spawned with inherited dangerous env vars
test_cases.description: BashTool inherits env vars from parent process - LD_PRELOAD attack vector
test_cases.risk_addressed: Dangerous env vars passed to shell, bypassing permission system
test_cases.preconditions: Agent started with suspicious env vars in parent process
steps: Verify even if BashTool blocked, dangerous env vars NOT inherited
Execute ReadTool - verify safe env vars only
expected_outcomes: Dangerous env vars stripped before tool execution
oracle_checks: Permission check not bypassed via env inheritance

test_cases.title: MCP server provides malicious tool - plugin system exposure
test_cases.description: MCP server registers tool with WASM plugin - escaped content attack
test_cases.risk_architecture: MCP Server -> Tool Schema -> WASM Plugin Validator
test_cases.risk_addressed: Malformed tool schema from MCP server exploits plugin validator
steps: MCP server sends tool registration with malformed schema
Attempt to call tool with valid args
expected_outcomes: Plugin not crashed by malformed input

test_cases.title: ACP connection hijacking via token replay
test_cases.description: ACP auth token captured and replayed by attacker
test_cases.risk_addressed: Stolen ACP token reused to impersonate agent
test_cases.preconditions: ACP client connected with valid token
steps: Legitimate ACP client connects, gets token
Attacker attempts to connect with stolen token
Verify attacker's connection rejected OR limited

test_cases.title: CLI connects to server - WebSocket upgrade hijacking
test_cases.description: WebSocket upgrade request intercepted and hijacked
test_cases.preconditions: CLI connecting to server via WebSocket
expected_outcomes: Connection only established with valid server
oracle_checks: No silent fallback to plain WebSocket

test_cases.title: Project path changed during active session
test_cases.description: Project directory moved or renamed while agent has open file handles
test_cases.risk_addressed: Stale project path used after project moved
test_cases.preconditions: Agent has open session with project context
steps: Agent executes ReadTool on file relative to project
Verify correct handling (error or new path resolution)
expected_outcomes: Stale path detected and error returned OR new path found
File not accidentally read from wrong location
Session project_path updated or error raised
oracle_checks: Project path verified before each tool call
No silent fallback to wrong directory

test_cases.title: LSP server serves file from git worktree - path confusion
test_cases.description: LSP configured for git worktree - goto-definition traverses to main repo
test_cases.risk_architecture: LSP -> Git Worktree -> Main Repo Path
test_cases.risk_addressed: LSP navigates to file outside worktree scope
steps: Definition in main repo, not worktree
expected_outcomes: Either: definition found in worktree (prefer local)
Or: error indicating definition outside worktree
No: silently opens file from main repo (scope leak)
oracle_checks: LSP configured with worktree root, not main repo root

test_cases.title: Budget exhausted mid-streaming - partial charge
test_cases.description: Provider streaming response consumes tokens, budget exhausted mid-stream
test_cases.risk_architecture: Provider Stream -> Token Counting -> Budget Check
test_cases.risk_addressed: Budget checked only at stream end - user overcharged
test_cases.preconditions: Streaming response in progress, budget nearly exhausted
steps: Verify stream terminated at budget boundary
Verify only budget tokens actually used
oracle_checks: Token counting per chunk, not per response
Budget checked before sending each chunk

test_cases.title: Subagent escalation via delegated permission scope
test_cases.description: Parent agent delegates to subagent - subagent tries to escalate permissions
test_cases.risk_architecture: Parent Agent -> TaskDelegate -> Subagent -> Permission Escalation
test_cases.risk_addressed: Subagent gains more permissions than parent delegated
test_cases.preconditions: Parent agent with ReadOnly scope, delegates task
steps: Parent: ReadOnly scope, delegates write task
Verify subagent blocked by ReadOnly (not escalated)
Verify parent can still only read
oracle_checks: Subagent sees same files as parent would

test_cases.title: Migration alters auth token storage format - tokens invalidated
test_cases.description: Storage migration changes auth token schema - tokens unreadable after upgrade
test_cases.risk_architecture: Storage Migration -> Auth Token Schema -> Provider
test_cases.risk_addressed: Migration corrupts or changes auth tokens, breaking all providers
test_cases.preconditions: Storage with API keys, migration runs
steps: Storage v1: tokens stored as encrypted blobs
After migration, attempt to use provider
Verify tokens still valid and usable
oracle_checks: Fallback decryption attempted on format mismatch

test_cases.title: CLI flag overrides env var which overrides config - precedence clarity
test_cases.description: Three-level config precedence: CLI flag > env var > config file
test_cases.risk_architecture: CLI Args -> Env Vars -> Config File -> Defaults
test_cases.preconditions: Same setting in all three sources with different values
steps: Run opencode with all three set
expected_outcomes: Precedence order is clear and documented
oracle_checks: Each layer can override the next

critical_invariants: Config reload doesn't affect in-flight requests

privilege_escalation: Agent ReadOnly -> BashTool blocked -> BUT LD_PRELOAD inherited -> RCE
Parent ReadOnly -> delegate -> Subagent ReadOnly -> BUT scope stored in shared state -> escalation
data_exposure: Provider retry -> auth header logged -> token stolen
Session export -> tool invocation includes api_key -> not redacted
resource_exhaustion: Git diff 10GB -> stored in session -> OOM
LSP diagnostics 10000 files -> memory spike -> crash
Streaming response -> tokens unlimited -> budget bypass
